reviews.review_description:
I just wanted to write this because I genuinely love the Retropolis games and wanted to show some appreciation while also sharing some feedback as a fan.

First of all, I don’t usually write reviews. The fact that you guys actually respond to players is what made me want to write this in the first place. Seeing developer replies under so many reviews was really nice to see, and it genuinely made me excited to share my thoughts because it felt like there was a good chance someone would actually read them.

That’s also one of the things I appreciate most about your team. It shows that you actually take the time to listen to players and care about what they have to say instead of just releasing a game and moving on. It’s something I always respect in developers.

As for the game itself, I absolutely love the atmosphere and style of Retropolis. The retro-futuristic aesthetic, the music, the characters, the animation, the city itself and everything just works so well together. It feels so refreshing, especially in VR. A lot of VR games don’t keep me interested for very long, but Retropolis was one of those games that genuinely pulled me into its world.

And to be honest, my biggest complaint is that I wanted more. I wasn’t satisfied when it ended, not because the ending was bad, but because I genuinely wanted to keep playing. I wanted to explore more of the city, discover more locations, learn more about the characters, and spend more time in that world.

One thing I’d really love to see is more of Philip’s backstory. I feel like there’s still so much to learn about him, and I’d love to understand him on a deeper level. I think seeing more of his past would help players connect with him emotionally and make us feel even more immersed in his character.

I’d also love to see more of Philip and Jenny. Maybe it’s just me, but at first I didn’t really understand that they were supposed to be ex-lovers. I thought it was more of a fling than a serious past relationship. I’d love to see more of their history together because I feel like there’s a lot there that could be explored.

Another thing I’d love is more choices throughout the game. Not just one major choice at the end, but choices from the beginning that actually affect different situations, conversations, relationships, or outcomes. I know that would probably be difficult to implement, but I think it would make the experience even more immersive and replayable.

My main gameplay feedback would be making objectives a bit clearer. There were moments where I found myself wandering around unsure of what I was supposed to do next, and I ended up needing walkthroughs or Youtube videos to continue. A little more guidance in those moments would definitely help.

Another thing I’d love to see is more freedom to explore the city and take part in side quests. I completely understand that Retropolis is a story-driven game and that the main objective is supposed to guide the player through the experience. However, there were a lot of times when I found myself wanting to step away from the main objective for a bit and just explore.

For example, if the main objective is finding a file in the police department, it would be fun if players had the option to temporarily leave that objective and pick up a small side quest, investigate a different mystery, or interact with other characters before continuing the main story. I think side stories like these would be a great way to naturally make the game longer while also helping players feel more connected to the city and its residents.

I’d especially love side stories that focus on the characters. More scenes between Philip and Jenny, more interactions with other characters, more background stories, and more opportunities to learn about the world. The city of Retropolis feels so interesting that I constantly wanted reasons to spend more time in it. Rather than simply making the main story longer, I’d love more side stories, optional detective cases, character interactions, and lore that encourage players to explore the world and get attached to its characters.

Overall, I genuinely think this series has so much potential. The world, characters, atmosphere, and style are already incredible, and I feel like there’s still so much more that could be done with them.

Thank you for creating a game that felt different from everything else I’ve played in VR. It’s honestly literally my favorite VR experiences, and the fact that my biggest complaint is that I wanted more says a lot.

I’m really excited to see what comes next, and hopefully we’ll get a Retropolis 3 someday. I’ll definitely be playing it if we do.

Thanks again for creating such an amazing series.

I love the art style and how well the game was put together, though it was significantly harder than the first. Might just be me being an idiot but I had to look up a lot of the puzzles because we weren’t given enough context or were just expected to figure out things without any information, which sounds like every puzzle game, but I felt lost quite a few times in my play through

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words about the art style. It means a lot to our team. You're not alone in finding this one tougher than the first. We've heard from a few players that some puzzles don't give enough signposting, and it's feedback we're taking seriously as we look at future updates. We'd love to know which moments felt most opaque if you're willing to share. That kind of specific detail really helps us figure out where to add a nudge or a bit more context.

reviews.review_description: I loved this game! If you're looking for a really good game with a puzzle at a medium difficulty level, this is for you. The atmosphere is addictive and you feel completely immersed in the main character's world. The story is super cool and funny. I've played both available games and I can't wait to play the next one when it's released!

developer_response.body: Thank you for review, and playing both games. We have more to tell in the world of Retropolis, though nothing is announced yet. In the meantime, if you want more to explore, our new game Riddlewood Manor is available now in early access on Meta Quest and we'd love to have you along as we build it out.

reviews.review_description:
I love to see a game by my Israeli fellows I was so surprised!!! 
This game is amazing and so underrated as a puzzle lover I can’t believe I’ve never heard of it. 
Better then red matter sorry red matter lovers tbh it’s better that any game puzzle I played on the vr

developer_response.body: This genuinely made our day, thank you. Hearing that it holds up against anything else in the genre means a lot to us. If you want more to explore, our new franchise Riddlewood Manor is in early access on Meta Quest and we'd love to have you along for the ride.

reviews.review_description: This game satisfies my desire to be a film noir detective. The puzzles were decently complex and fun to figure out. I absolutely love the dialogue and characters. Can’t wait for the next part to come out! One thing I would like more is if I was able to walk around instead of being stuck in one spot while I look around for the next clues, but i’m being nit picky lol.

developer_response.body: Being your go-to film noir detective fix is exactly what we set out to do, so this means a lot. The locomotion feedback is noted, and we understand why full freedom of movement would feel satisfying in a world like Retropolis. There's plenty more story we want to tell in this universe, and while we can't confirm what's next just yet, if you haven't explored The Secret of Retropolis you've got another case waiting. We also just launched Riddlewood Manor in early access on Meta Quest, a ghost-hunting point-and-click mystery worth checking out 👻

reviews.review_title: This is top 3 vr games oat
reviews.review_description: This vr game has such a plot twist it is amazing I can’t fathom how good this game is it’s so good worth every dollar I spent you need to get this game and play part 1 first.

developer_response.body: That plot twist gets people every time (: we genuinely love hearing that reaction. Thank you for the kind words and for pointing new players toward Part 1 first; that's exactly how it's meant to be experienced. Reviews like yours mean a lot to a small team. See you in our next game!

reviews.review_title: Best vr game I’ve ever played
reviews.review_description:
I really hope yall make a third game, I watched the trailer and was scared to buy the game I bought it anyway and it is now one of my favorite vr games ever I literally can’t find a better game
PLEASE MAKE A THIRD GAME🙏🙏🙏

developer_response.body: That genuinely means a lot to us, and we love that you took the leap despite the nerves. We have more stories to tell in this world, though nothing we can announce just yet. In the meantime, our new franchise Riddlewood Manor is in early access on Meta Quest if you're looking for your next adventure.

reviews.review_description: The clown level on floor 2 of the lighthouse is in a rigged-lose state on my game. Ive watched 10 different youtube videos of people passing that, but after more than 100 attempts following specific instruction, I've realized that my game has a bug and is in a rigged lose state. Waste of money. Even if it was working, it's nowhere near as good as other VR games. Save your money and play "I Expect You to Die" instead. Better graphics, story, gameplay, everything.

developer_response.body:
I am so incredibly sorry for the frustration this has caused you. Hearing that you’ve tried over and over after watching tutorials makes it clear something is seriously wrong, and that is definitely not the experience we want for you.

We would love the chance to help you get past this, whether that means investigating the bug or sending you a custom save file with that specific puzzle already solved so you can continue the story.

Please reach out to us at support@peanutbutton.net so we can get this sorted for you right away.

reviews.review_description: The first game built a world, this one tore it apart and put it back together again. Intriguing story, challenging puzzles (enough to be exciting not too much so as to be frustrating), and characters you truly care about. This game is impossible to put down. The hand painted environments and aesthetic of this game are incredible. The amount of time and effort put into this game clearly comes across in its seamless execution! The only thing I’d suggest is a hint button for players who are more into this kind of story than puzzle games, but for me it was perfect. Beware it ends on a cliffhanger!!! I just hope a 3rd game is made soon! This is incredible!!!

developer_response.body: That cliffhanger gets  jupeople every time, and we love that you felt it so deeply. We also noted your feedback on hints - suprer valid! We have more to tell in the world of Retropolis, though nothing is announcedst yet. In the meantime, if you're looking for your next fix, we just launched Riddlewood Manor in early access on Quest this month and would love to have you along for that journey too.

reviews.review_title: Peak story and a Wonderfull game
reviews.review_description:
4 Episodes feel small, But it takes well around 3-4 hours. Quite an enjoyable expirience If you like puzzle games. Wonderfull art style in general. 
It got funny jokes and pretty good characters too.(The plot twist was crazy)
Also Betty2 carried The story We need More of her

developer_response.body: Betty2 carrying the story is something we hear a lot, and honestly, we're not surprised. Really glad the plot twist landed the way it did. If you want more to explore while we keep working on what comes next in Retropolis, our early access title Riddlewood Manor is out now on Meta Quest and would love some company.

reviews.review_description: This game was actually amazing no cap I loved it. It’s very engaging and grasps your attention. Highly recommend this game!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much, this genuinely made us smile! If you want more to explore in this world, The Secret of Retropolis is set in the same universe and happens before this one. And if you're up for something new, our latest game Riddlewood Manor is now in early access on Meta Quest.

reviews.review_description: Great fun with some challenging problems. Built-in walkthrough much appreciated but would much rather have hints/tips (with increasing amounts of help) rather than a 'spoiler' walkthrough. Looking forward to the next installment.

developer_response.body: Really glad the walkthrough helped you through the tricky spots, and the hint system idea is one we genuinely love. Layered hints that give you just enough to keep going without spoiling the moment is exactly the kind of thing we'd want too. We have more to tell in the world of Retropolis, though nothing is announced yet. We're also building out Riddlewood Manor in early access on Quest if you want something to explore in the meantime.

reviews.review_description: If you liked the first opus you'll love this one

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! If the first game clicked with you, we hope this one delivered. If you're looking for your next adventure, our new franchise Riddlewood Manor is in early access on Meta Quest right now and we'd love to have you along for the ride as we build it out.

reviews.review_description:
I just finished Retropolis 2: Never Say Goodbye, and it is easily a 5-star experience for me. Here is why it stands out:
​The Vibe: The hand-painted art style looks incredible in the headset. It nails that moody, cynical noir atmosphere but with a cast of robots that brings the world to life.
​The Puzzles: The "brain games" are genuinely interesting. They aren't just generic logic puzzles; they feel integrated into the world and the detective work. They made me feel smart without making me want to rage-quit.
​The Writing: The script is fantastic. It balances the mystery with plenty of dark humor and sarcasm. The voice acting really sells the characters.
​Verdict: It is very entertaining and keeps you hooked the whole way through. Highly recommended

developer_response.body:
It is great to see you back for the sequel! 🤖 We’re thrilled that the hand-painted environments and the cynical humor landed so well for you. Our goal was to make the detective work feel seamless, so hearing that the puzzles made you feel like a pro is a huge win for us.

Thank you for the high recommendation and for following the series! To see what the Peanut Button team is cooking up next, we’d love to have you in our Discord or on our mailing list.

reviews.review_description:
Hi, I was really enjoying this game but it keeps crashing when I start it up so I'm unable to play it? Can anyone help?


**Great response and support from developers**

developer_response.body: Really glad the crash issue is behind you now, and thank you for updating your review after we pushed that patch. We hope you enjoy the rest of the game!

reviews.review_description: Giving this 5 stars because I really like the story line the first game was cool liked the twist at the end where John was the magician but I didn't really like the ending that a real cliffhanger right there I really thought we would get Jenny back but most over the game was amazing but I wish there was more

developer_response.body:
It's so rewarding to see that kind of enthusiasm! We're thrilled you loved the story and the twists... Even if that cliffhanger left you wanting more. We definitely wanted to keep the tension high, and hearing that you wish there was more is the best feedback we could ask for.

We would love to have you in our community to share your theories on what happens next and to stay close to the studio's journey!

Join our Discord or sign up for our newsletter!

reviews.review_title: One of the best story driven VR games
reviews.review_description:
The first game plays like a point-and-click adventure. But this one is more like puzzle game! The story and voice acting are among the best in VR games! The graphics are very nice. The main character and his sense of humor encourage you to click everywhere and explore every nook and cranny again and again, if only to listen to his amusing monologues.

I’m writing this review after finishing both parts! The first part is a solid introduction. The second one is even better - it’s much more advanced and with a richer and longer story.

developer_response.body:
It means the world to us that you played through both games and took the time to review them both! Knowing that you found the second chapter to be an even richer, more advanced experience is exactly what we strive for.

We’re so glad you enjoyed Philip’s monologues and that the humor kept you clicking on every little detail. We would love to have you in our community to share your journey and stay close to the studio as we continue our own!

Join our Discord or sign up for our newsletter!

reviews.review_description:
I found that the puzzles-escape room style of games are the ones I enjoy most.
This game is definitely different from the other games I've played so far.
I think the main uniqueness of this game is the writing and atmosphere, reminded me a bit portal 2, in terms that it also made me laugh while playing.
There were 2-3 riddles that I needed help with, more than the usual games I played so far, but you can compare it for hints during escape room, that some rooms are harder than others, so overall most of the puzzles can be resolved without help.
The retroplis-polis ringtone is genius.

developer_response.body:
It’s a huge compliment to be compared to Portal 2. Especially when it comes to the humor! We’re thrilled that the writing and atmosphere hit that sweet spot for you.

We also love that you caught the "Retropolis-police" jingle! We had a blast putting that together. Regarding the puzzles, it's totally normal to need a "hint" now and then, just like a real escape room :)

We’d love to have you in our community to hear which specific joke or puzzle was your favorite!

Join our Discord or sign up for our newsletter!

reviews.review_title: I'll be back, for all sequels
reviews.review_description:
I give this game five stars. I was thinking of deducting one star because I can't find a way to lower the volume of the music. A volume slider or a simple checkbox for music on/off seems like something every game should have. When trying to solve a problem or thinking of what to do next I find the music disturbing. 
The game is very funny though and definitely a five star game, so I let it... slide. Who'd knew a wrench could be that helpful. Now I never leave home without one. 
I can't wait for Retropolis 3 featuring my favorite robotcop and hopefully a volume control.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your review, Inkfish. We definitely hear you on the volume control! It’s great to know the wrench and the humor kept you hooked despite the music. We'd love to have you in our community to hear more of your thoughts while we work on what's next for our favorite robot cop!

Join our Discord or sign up for our newsletter!

reviews.review_title: Much better than the first version
reviews.review_description: I bought the first version and requested a refund after completing it in about 20 minutes. Picked this up on quest + and it’s so much better than the first one. Hours of game play in this version. Humour still on point, and a fun story. I love point and click adventures and this is probably the best one on quest for me.

developer_response.body:
It is great to see you gave us another shot after the first game! We’re so glad you found the longer gameplay and story more satisfying this time around—it’s a huge compliment to be called your favorite point-and-click on the Quest.

We’d love to have you in our community to chat about your favorite moments or what you'd like to see next!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description: Just started playing this one.  Awesomeness in the storyline and the puzzles!  I like that you can get access to the walkthrough for help.  The storyline transitions were just like a movie.  The sarcasm is a hoot.  Well recommend.

developer_response.body:
It is fantastic to hear that the cinematic transitions and the sarcasm are keeping you hooked! We’re so glad you’re finding the walkthrough helpful for those trickier puzzles as you dive into the mystery.

We’d love to have you in our community to hear your thoughts as you get deeper into the story!

Join our Discord or sign up for our newsletter!

reviews.review_description: The story is really well written, and was an amazing overall experience. Its not often a vr game succeeds in creating an engaging vr game that doesn't make sensitive users motion-sick. I reccommend this game overall. I would like to see hand tracking support, as that would really complete the immersion.

developer_response.body:
It means a lot to hear that the story and experience felt so seamless for you! We put a lot of care into making the movement comfortable for everyone, so it’s rewarding to know you enjoyed the ride without any motion sickness.

Your suggestion for hand tracking, as we did for Retropolis 1, is fantastic - immersion is everything in Retropolis, and we’re always looking for ways to make the City of Robots feel more real. We'd love to have you in our community to hear more of your ideas!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description:
A fantastic narrative VR experience with a unique retro-futuristic style.
Retropolis delivers a clever mix of storytelling, puzzles and atmosphere across two episodes. A must-play for fans of story-driven VR games.

developer_response.body: Hi Majerik, a huge thank you for playing both Retropolis games and for the wonderful feedback! We noticed you left this review for both titles, and it truly means a lot to us that you stuck with the story. Hearing that you enjoyed the retro-futuristic style and the puzzles is exactly why we make these games. Thanks for being part of the Retropolis world! We'd love to have you in our community! Join our Discord or sign up for our newsletter to see what's next from us!

reviews.review_description: this was a wonderful continuation of the first game, but my absolute favorite part of the first one was the hand tracking, it worked perfectly for the point and click gameplay and really made it feel like his hands were my own! i truly hope that it's something they add in later! it adds so much to the immersion, and i would 100% replay it if they did :) retropolis is such a refreshing game to have in vr, it's so different from all the other genres that creators tend to use and i hope to see more like it. thank you for creating such a beautiful little world <3

developer_response.body:
It’s so good to see you back in Retropolis! Thank you for the kind words about the atmosphere and for your honest feedback regarding hand tracking. We know how much that immersion meant to you in the first game!

While we don't have hand tracking in the sequel just yet, we are always listening to our players as we plan our next moves. We have plenty more stories to tell in this world, and we'd love for you to help shape them! Please join our Discord or sign up for our mailing list so you can stay updated on any future features and be the first to know what Peanut Button is working on next! <3

reviews.review_description: So you play this right after you finish the first game because you want more :) And this game delivers! A longer game, just as fun as the first one, if not even better. Gameplay is easy to understand, and the story is interesting as well. Can't wait for their next game! כל הכבוד! ממש אהבתי את המשחק!

developer_response.body:
It means the world to us that you jumped straight into this second chapter! We worked hard to make sure this journey felt like a true evolution, so hearing that the expanded scope hit the mark is fantastic.

While we aren't ready to pull back the curtain on a next entry just yet, there are certainly many more stories waiting to be told in the world of Retropolis. We'd love to have you with us as we figure out what's next!

!כל הכבוד על הסיום, שמחנו מאוד לקרוא את המילים שלך

reviews.review_description:
This game maybe the best one ive played in a long time great story line great tracking. And really cool art style and i love how much interactions you can have with the characters.  I need a 3d one pleaseee 😝

ill  Definitely  buy the 3d episode when it comes out 😄

developer_response.body:
It's so rewarding to hear that the character interactions and art style resonated with you so deeply! We’re thrilled you’re already looking forward to what’s next.

We'd love to have you in our community to share more of your ideas for the future of the series!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description: I loved the first one, and this one is even better. I don't think I've ever felt so immersed; so much of that had to do with the work that went into the storyline, puzzles, and character development. I love the little puns (like in the bar) and the way it feels like you are stepping into a film. I'd love to know some of what influenced the style and story, I'm so inspired by this team and final product. Thank you for tinging me something to look forward to at the end of the day ❤️

developer_response.body:
Hearing that our world became the highlight of your day is exactly why we make games. We put a lot of focus into making the atmosphere feel like a living, breathing noir film, so it's wonderful to see those small details and puns resonating with you.

As for our influences, we'd be happy to dive into that with you! Come chat with us on Discord or shoot us a message through our website. We'd love to geek out over the art and lore that shaped this universe. There is still so much of this city left to explore, and while a new chapter hasn't been announced yet, the gears are always turning.

reviews.review_description: Loved it, still want to love it, but can't. There's a bug in part 4 involving hats (to keep it vague for those that don't have this issue) that will not allow you to do what you need to do in order to progress. Thankfully this game was free at the time and I really was truly enjoying it, but then I got slapped in the face. Thanks for wasting an afternoon.

developer_response.body: Really glad the game hooked you before things went sideways, and we're sorry the afternoon ended on a frustrating note. That hat interaction in Episode 4 is a tricky one. it requires pointing your hand directly at the hat, which can feel unintuitive. If you drop us a line at support@peanutbutton.net we can walk you through it, or send over a save file that gets you past that point so you can see how the story ends.

reviews.review_description: This whole series has been my all time favorite game on VR so far. The story is captivating and the puzzles are a delight to figure out. The voice acting is top notch and his voice is so pleasing, I can listen to it all day.

developer_response.body:
It is so rewarding to hear that we've become your all-time favorite VR series! We're thrilled that the story and puzzles have captivated you, and we'll be sure to pass along the high praise to our voice cast. It’s a huge compliment to know Philip's voice is one you could listen to all day.

We'd love to have you in our community to share that enthusiasm with other fans!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description:
I have only 1 question, when do we go to the movies?  After buying Retropolis 1&2 I am throughly hooked.  These games are so fun and entertaining, a little bit of brain twisting and dark humour keeps you intrigued and eager to carry on.

I NEED RETROPOLIS 3 NOWWWW!

developer_response.body:
You caught that cliffhanger! We love that you're ready to follow Philip and Betty2 straight into the studios. It’s so rewarding to know the dark humor and puzzles have you totally hooked for the next chapter.

We’d love to have you in our community to hear what's next from our studio.

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description: I played the first and this one, I gotta say, probably the best game I ever played in my whole life, amazing story, amazing comedy, great puzzles. I might be a bit of a crybaby but yea.i cried. I definitely would want more games like this, I'm a story freak. I was so immersed I forgot I wasn't him. Everything is gentle on the eyes, i love the designs as well. I love Betty!!

developer_response.body: This absolutely made our day. Knowing you’ve been with us since the first game and felt that deeply connected to the world we built is the highest compliment we could get. We put a lot of soul into these characters, so hearing they resonated with you (and even tugged at the heartstrings!) means everything. We’d actually love to have you over in our Discord community. It’s full of fellow story lovers, and we think you’d fit right in with the rest of the crew. Thanks for your support.

reviews.review_description: The story was beautiful and creative and the puzzles were just a little too difficult, just enough to be challenging. Which was nice.  The art was great!  Out of all the games I’ve played I’m most excited about this one!! I really hope they continue the story with a retropolis 3!!!!

developer_response.body:
It means the world to hear that out of everything you’ve played, our world is the one that has you most excited! We’re so happy the puzzles provided that perfect "aha!" moment for you.

We would love to have you in our community to share that energy and keep the conversation going!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_title: Excellent sequel to an amazing game!!!!!
reviews.review_description:
I just finished this game—wow!! I absolutely loved the first one, and this sequel truly lived up to it. I was pulled into the story, the visuals, and the overall experience from start to finish.

The storytelling, writing, and atmosphere were outstanding, and every part of the game felt carefully crafted. It was an incredible experience that left me wanting more—please tell me there’s another one coming!

I highly recommend this game and the first one to everyone. The comfort level in VR is excellent, and the whole experience is charming, engaging, and immersive. To everyone who worked on this game: you did an amazing job!

developer_response.body:
It is so rewarding to see you cross the finish line! We remember you just starting this journey a few days ago, and seeing that the sequel hit those same "wow" notes for you is exactly why we keep building this world.

You mentioned the comfort level and the craftsmanship—that's something the Peanut Button team takes very seriously, so hearing it means a lot. As for that "wanting more" feeling, we aren't ready to say goodbye to this universe just yet!

To stay close to the source for what's brewing next, come hang out with us in the Discord or join the mailing list. Thanks for being such a champion for both games!

reviews.review_title: Gets everything right except one thing.
reviews.review_description:
Beautiful art, crime noir, what I'm sure is a compelling story...

Awful, awful voice acting for the main character. I mean *TERRIBLE*. The fact that it's who you play, and who narrates, makes it worse.

developer_response.body: We really appreciate the honest take. It’s a bummer that the voice acting pulled you out of an atmosphere you otherwise enjoyed, especially with the art and noir vibe. Feedback like this is exactly what helps us level up for the future. We’d love to have you in our community to hear more of your thoughts as we keep growing!

reviews.review_description:
PROS: Intriguing story + Good voices + Lot of puzzles + Funny and easy to play + Music

CONS: not fluent animations + A bit short + It doesn't have a final (its go on)

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the detailed breakdown! It’s great to hear the music, voices, and humor hit the mark for you. We appreciate the honest feedback on the animations and the "to be continued" ending—we're always working to polish the experience as the story evolves.

We’d love to have you in our community to share more of your thoughts as we build what’s next!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description: Wont play, tried uninstalling, still wont load. Keeps closing itself.

developer_response.body:
**update - April 26**
Hi again, we've patched the game to solve the crash issue. Please update to the newest version, try relaunching, and let us know if it worked for you, or if there's anything else needed. Thank you.

**original response**
I am so sorry to hear you’re running into these crashes. That sounds incredibly frustrating, especially when you’ve already tried the uninstall/reinstall dance. We want to get you back into the City of Robots as quickly as possible!

Please reach out to us at support@peanutbutton.net so we can take a closer look at what’s happening and help you fix this.

reviews.review_description: I enjoyed this so much that I was sad it was over. Very challenging and engaging. Everything working like it should, and the scenery was fun. Had a few laughs, too. I’ll be waiting for the next one in this series.

developer_response.body:
Welcome back, Rojoski58! It’s so good to see you again after you enjoyed the first chapter.

We’re thrilled that the challenges and scenery in this longer journey hit the mark for you. Knowing it gave you a few laughs makes the work all the more worth it! Thank you so much for playing and for sharing your thoughts again.

We’d love to have you in our community while you wait for what's next!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description: These are great games and everything ABIUT it is great but I finished the 1st one in 1 day and a half and the other one - 1 day. I want one that isn’t short

developer_response.body:
It is awesome to see you powered through both stories so quickly! We definitely heard the feedback on length after the first game, which is why we made this sequel a much beefier experience.

While we don't have a third game to announce right now, we hear you loud and clear on wanting something even more substantial. We aren't done with the city of robots yet—there are plenty of gears still turning behind the scenes.

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter to stay updated on our next big move.

reviews.review_description: I played the first game and it was amazing then I see there is a second game and I downloaded it immediately and I love the art style the Devs went with. To be honest I don't really like point and click games but this was a amazing experience, I would recommend this for vr Story players. I hope that the Devs keep making amazing games like this and I hope that a third game will come soon!

developer_response.body:
It’s so cool to hear that we managed to win over a non-point-and-click fan! 🤖 We’re thrilled that you jumped straight into the sequel after the first game. That’s the ultimate compliment.

We definitely plan on keeping the games coming. To stay updated on what the Peanut Button team is dreaming up next, come hang out in our Discord or join the mailing list. Thank you again :)

reviews.review_description:
Finished the first one and immediately jumped into this one. The emotional beats hit so well, loved the mirror moments in the office. The comedy was done great, don't mess with the Betty's stuff!! The story unfolded in a way where you wanted to see what was going to happen next. We need more adventures with Philip!!

Also, Philip was right it does get stuck in your head.. Teriyaki-tech!!

If there is ever a physical vinyl of the soundtrack, I'd buy it in a second!!

developer_response.body:
It’s awesome to see you finished the case! 🤖 We’re so glad the "Betty" humor and those emotional office moments resonated with you—Betty 2 sends her regards! Sorry about the "Teriyaki-tech" earworm, though...

A physical vinyl is a dream of ours, too! In the meantime, the soundtrack is available on Spotify and Apple Music if you want to keep the vibes going. To see what’s next for Philip and the Peanut Button team, join our Discord or mailing list. Thanks for the incredible support!

reviews.review_title: Excellent follow-up to the first game.
reviews.review_description:
I just finished this sequel and was very impressed. The story, visuals, atmosphere, and characters were all on point. The dialogue was quirky and funny, with great tone and timing. Jokes landed and then moved out of the way so you could get on with the mystery (and it *was* a mystery, which I appreciated).

The puzzles were challenging enough to make you think without being frustrating, and more than once I was slightly embarrassed when I finally “got it” and realized I’d missed an obvious clue. The only exception was the “Simon” puzzles — those were a bit much for me, but my goldfish brain has never handled those well.

All in all, this was a fun, well-crafted game, and I’m glad I played it. I really hope they make a third one.

developer_response.body:
It’s wonderful to see you back for the sequel! 🤖 We’re so glad you enjoyed the mystery and the humor this time around. Those memory puzzles can definitely be a challenge, so we appreciate you sticking with them to see the story through!

We love your enthusiasm for a third game. To stay in the loop and share more of your thoughts with the Peanut Button team, join our Discord or mailing list.

reviews.review_description: I LOVED all 4 episodes, I CANNOT wait for more in the future, I really hope they continue it, all the characters had great personalities, Everything was amazing, loved betty2 (play the game the know who that is) everything was SO GOOD

developer_response.body:
We’re so happy you loved all four episodes! 🤖 Hearing that the characters and story resonated with you is the best feedback we could ask for. Betty 2 sends her regards—she’s glad she has a fan in you!

We definitely have more stories to tell in this universe. To be the first to know what's next for Philip and the rest of the crew, as well as other Peanut Button adventures, join the Peanut Button Discord or mailing list. We can't wait to see you in the next adventure!

reviews.review_description: When I tell you that my jaw dropped on this storyline! Devs, please tell me part 3 is in the making! Fantastic work on this one and Part 1 !!

developer_response.body:
It’s fantastic to see you’ve already completed the second case! 🤖 Hearing that the plot twist took you by surprise is a huge compliment to our writing team.

While we aren't ready to share specific details about a third installment just yet, the support from players like you is exactly what keeps the Peanut Button team inspired to tell more stories in this universe. To be the first to hear about our future projects, come join the conversation on our Discord or mailing list. Thanks for sticking with the series!

reviews.review_description: I personally like the first better, but i cant hate this game, because the issues i had with it were my headset acting wierd and being to dumb to do the puzzles. But cant wait till number 3 if there is going to be one, please do it like this games format.

developer_response.body:
It’s great to see you back after your "infinity billion stars" review of the first game! 🤖 We’re sorry to hear about the headset issues, and don't worry—the puzzles in this one were definitely designed to be a step up in difficulty!

We love that you want a third game in this format. We still have plenty of stories to tell in this world, so make sure to join our Discord or mailing list to stay updated on what Peanut Button is working on next. Thanks for staying such a passionate fan!

reviews.review_description:
Oh man, this was difficult. Didn’t expect that. Lucky there was the walkthrough to check when you were stuck. 
Much more puzzles then Retropolis 1. 

Loved playing both.

developer_response.body:
It’s great to see you made it through! We really wanted to raise the stakes and give players something to chew on this time around, so we're glad the challenge felt like a step up. We’re thrilled you enjoyed the journey across both games.

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter to stay tuned for what's next!

reviews.review_description:
The art style, the voice acting, the humor and the puzzles all fit so well together. I could barely put my headset down until I finished it. The first Retropolis game was just as amazing, only shorter, like an appetizer for this wonderful piece of work. 
   I cannot wait for future games from this dev to release. They’ll be a “must buy” for me.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the incredible review! We love the "appetizer" comparison—it’s exactly how we hoped the transition from the first game to Never Say Goodbye would feel. Knowing that our work has become a "must buy" for you is the ultimate motivation for our team.

We aren't done with the world of Retropolis just yet! If you want to stay in the loop on our future projects and see what’s cooking at Peanut Button, please join our Discord or sign up for our mailing list. We’d love to have you with us for the next adventure!

reviews.review_description: This game is hilarious funnier than the first one again quite short.Good puzzles,story is good .I thoroughly enjoyed playing it.

developer_response.body:
Welcome back, Mark0000! It’s great to see you again.

We’re so glad you found this one even funnier than the first. Making people laugh is what we aim for! Thank you so much for playing through the sequel and sharing your feedback on the puzzles and story.

We'd love to have you in our community to stay in the loop!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description: Everything about the game is amazing (except for the puzzles) I liked the last games puzzles a lot more these ones just seem convoluted thought all things considered I’m definitely getting the third game when it comes out

developer_response.body: Thanks for the support and for sticking with us! 🤖 We’re sorry the puzzles felt a bit convoluted this time—we really appreciate that honest feedback. Since you’re already looking forward to a third game, we’d love to have you in our Discord or mailing list. It’s the best place to share your thoughts with the Peanut Button team and hear what’s coming next!

reviews.review_description: Very good game, would appreciate if you added extras like in the first game

developer_response.body:
Thanks for coming back for the sequel! We're so glad you're still with us.

We really appreciate the feedback on the extras—it's great to know what players miss from the first one. We’d love to have you in our community to hear more of your ideas!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description: The first game was awesome and this one can’t wait for part three

developer_response.body: Thank you! Really glad both games hit the mark for you. We have more stories to tell in this world, though nothing to announce just yet. In the meantime, our new franchise Riddlewood Manor is in early access on Meta Quest if you want something new to explore.

reviews.review_description:
The game was more challanging and i did need to use the in game help mechanic a few times but the story was way better with the twists and turns and multiput suprsing moments i was praying for a third game to be innthe works right after the credits rolled

the time was longer but the happines was greater

developer_response.body:
It is wonderful to see you back for the second chapter! 🤖 We are so glad the new twists and turns kept you guessing until the very end. We actually designed the help mechanic specifically to keep the mystery moving, so we’re happy it came in handy during those tougher sections!

Hearing that the longer experience brought even more enjoyment is the best feedback we could ask for. While we continue to look toward the future of the series, we would love to have you in our Discord or mailing list to stay updated on everything Peanut Button is building. Thanks for supporting the journey!

reviews.review_description: Some things take some fiddling around . The story is really cool!

developer_response.body:
We are so sorry to hear you got stuck! We designed that storage room so you could place items back where you found them until you reunite with your inventory, but if that isn’t working for you, it sounds like you may have encountered a specific bug.

Before you give up on Chapter 2, please do try to return the taser to the locker cell, and if it doesn't work, please reach out to our support at support@peanutbutton.net or find us on Discord. If you can send a short video of the issue, it would help us fix it for everyone. We’d also be happy to send you a save file so you can skip right past that section and finish the story! 🤖

reviews.review_description: CRAZY PLOT way better than the first in my opinion clever puzzles but some kinda hard to find out but yeah if the game is on sale i recomend it looking foward to the third one

developer_response.body:
We love hearing that the plot twist got you! 🤖 It’s awesome to know you enjoyed this one even more than the first. Those puzzles were definitely designed to be a bit more of a "brain-burner," so great job for seeing them through!

We’re already looking forward to the future, too. If you haven't already, join our Discord or mailing list to stay tuned for what’s next from Peanut Button. Thanks for the high recommendation!

reviews.review_description:
Really enjoyed this, i'm going to say it was better than the original. Better games & puzzles and a great story, feel & artwork.

Looing forward to a 3rd instalment

developer_response.body:
It’s so good to see you back in Retropolis! 🤖 I’m really glad we were able to get that promo code sorted over email so you could dive into the sequel. Hearing that you enjoyed the puzzles and artwork even more than the original makes it all worth it!

We love the energy for a third installment. To stay in the loop with the team and be the first to hear what we’re working on next, come join us in the Discord or on our mailing list Thanks for sticking with us!

reviews.review_description: top quality game

developer_response.body:
Great to see you again! We really appreciate the shout-out—it means a lot coming from someone who’s been through both games.

We'd love to have you in our community to see what we're building next!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description: Great game! I wish there was a 3rd! I love the story and the puzzles. Can't wait to see what they come up with next!

developer_response.body:
We’re so glad you loved the puzzles and the story! It’s hard to say "goodbye" to Retropolis. But don't worry! The city still has plenty of secrets left. 🤖

If you haven't already, join our Discord or mailing list to be the first to know what Peanut Button is coming up with next. We can't wait to share our future adventures with you!

reviews.review_description: This is a great game like the first one, you have to use what's around you to help. Never thought my own friend would turn out to be the Magician

developer_response.body:
It’s great to see you back in Retropolis! 🤖 We’re so glad you enjoyed the observation aspect of the gameplay again. And about the twist: you're right! It sure caught a few people off guard! 🎩

We have plenty more stories to tell in this world. To see what Peanut Button is working on next and join the discussion, come hang out in our Discord or sign up for our mailing list.

reviews.review_description: ...keep it going! 👍🏻

developer_response.body:
We’re so glad you enjoyed the leap from the original to this new chapter.

The team is definitely keeping the momentum up. We'd love for you to be part of the journey as we move forward!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter to see what’s coming next.

reviews.review_description: Love it. Good continuation. Would love to see more. Going to replay again soon

developer_response.body: It’s great to see you here too! We’re so happy the sequel lived up to your expectations. 🤖 Since you’re a pro at replaying these, we'd love to have you in our Discord or mailing list to hear your thoughts on what we should do next!

reviews.review_title: Grate game pls make a third one
reviews.review_description: When I played it it was grate funny but “spoiler warning “ the end cutscene was very gory and￼ if you are younger I don’t suggest this game tell they are a little older but it was grate. Ps pls in the next game add choices that kill Philip￼￼￼ and free roam👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻￼￼

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing and for the honest "spoiler warning" for other players! We're glad you enjoyed the humor, and we've definitely noted your ideas.

We’d love to have you in our community to hear more of your suggestions!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description: It is hard to tell which one is better, the first or the second episode. I hope episode 3 will come rather sooner than later. It will deffinately be a day one purchase for me!

developer_response.body: So glad to hear you’ve enjoyed both chapters! There's so much more we want to share from this world, and your excitement fuels us.

reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed the first one but this was just fantastic I absolutely loved it - really hoping there’s a third!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, Reid4! It’s amazing to hear that you enjoyed the sequel even more than the first. While we haven't announced a third game yet, we have so many more stories we want to tell in the world of Retropolis.

If you want to be the first to hear what’s next from the Peanut Button team, we’d love to have you in our Discord or on our mailing list!

reviews.review_description: This was such a fun game. I am 66 and I really enjoyed it. I got stuck a few times but for the most part you just have to think really hard. hahhaha I would defiantly tell people to get this game. Dont rush through it.  Enjoy the game
Great game hope to see a third and i love the refrence to the title of the game when one of them tries to say goodbye

developer_response.body:
We are so glad you caught that title reference! It’s awesome to hear you enjoyed the ride and are already looking forward to a third. Thank you so much for playing and for sharing your thoughts with us.

We would love to have you in our community to stay in the loop on what’s next!

Join our Discord and sign up for the newsletter!

reviews.review_description: Absolutely fantastic game. The design and style is very attractive. Narrative is great and perfect continuation from the first one. Recommended!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much! We’re thrilled you loved the style and narrative continuation. 🤖 Since you enjoyed the story so far, we’d love to have you join us for the next chapter of the Peanut Button journey.

If you haven't already, join our Discord or mailing list to be the first to hear about our future stories in the world of Retropolis.

reviews.review_description: Quality story-driven puzzle game.

I loved this game! Everything about it is polished and smooth. The mystery is involving, and the characters and voice acting is consistently strong. The game is set in a funky retro futuristic blend of '40's noir and colorful robots. The animation is rich with some surprising VR effects. The puzzles can be tough, but they are always fair with a satisfying solution.

Most importantly, the game is a lot of fun.  Everything that was good in the first game is greatly expanded in this sequel.  Now that I've finished it, I miss the characters and their world. Just spectacular!

developer_response.body: This was such a joy to read. Thank you, Zardog! We poured a lot of love into deepening the everything in the second game, so hearing it all landed so well means the world. We miss the characters too (believe us!)-and while we’re currently cooking up a whole new adventure, we hope you’ll join us for the ride. The doors of Retropolis may open again… 🕵️‍♂️✨ Join our Discord or mailing list to stay close.

reviews.review_title: just an episode, not a whole game
reviews.review_description:
I enjoyed the atmosphere and the puzzles, think Point & Click is way too underrepresented in VR, but although the game is long enough (needed about 8 hours - more than one of it for one specific puzzle, but still ok) I don't like the open end. Generally I can't accept the practice to split games into pieces and sell each as a whole game (regarding the price and how it is advertised).
It seemlessly starts where part one left and ends with clear need for a sequel but it seems that will never come as it is still not announced yet. So it leaves me with ambivalent feelings as the game itself was fun as far as it got.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing such thoughtful feedback, babubi76. We totally hear you - the way Retropolis 2 wraps up reflects how much more we wanted to tell in this story. While it's true that we haven’t announced a continuation just yet, Retropolis is very close to our hearts, and we’re grateful it found players like you who appreciate what it’s trying to do.

In the meantime, we’re pouring that same care into a brand-new adventure in a new franchise-and we’d love to keep you in the loop. Join our Discord or mailing list to stay connected. And thanks again for sticking with us through both episodes.

reviews.review_description: U cant expect to keep jenny waiting on her hero and stay clutched in the evil magician's hands please let us know when u make it id love to keep this franchise going. 🥰

developer_response.body: Thanks for the love, Nuria! While we’re working on something new at the moment, we’re just as excited to return to Retropolis down the line. Join our Discord or mailing list to be the first to hear when the next chapter takes shape 🖤✨

reviews.review_description: This was so fun to play,  i had to cheat a few times because I just couldn't figure out a puzzle 😅... I'm going to play it again.

developer_response.body: So glad to hear you had a good time in Retropolis, odonnarake! 😄 You're definitely not alone on those puzzles - happens even to the best detectives sometimes. Have fun on your second run, and if you haven’t already, come say hi in our Discord — we’d love to hear what details you spot on the next playthrough, and hear your thoughts on our upcoming game!

reviews.review_description:
This game has convinced me that classic point and click adventures belong in VR. The artwork and animations were pretty good and the voice acting not bad. 

The game was long enough, the set pieces and locations were well done. The humor and character development charming. Great vibes. 

Out of the many jank VR games this isn’t one of them.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for tsuch a complementing review, Five_Star_Man!
It means a lot to hear that Retropolis helped make the case for point & click in VR. We really focused on polish, charm, and building a world worth spending time in, so your words hit home. We’re deep into our next project now. It's something new, but with that same attention to storytelling and vibe. Hope to see you along for the ride! 💫 Join our Discord or mailing list to stay in the loop.

reviews.review_description: Love the gameplay. References to the past. Just an all out fun, problem solving game. Come test your knowledge...

developer_response.body: We’re thrilled you enjoyed the nostalgic vibes and puzzle-solving challenge — it’s exactly the kind of experience we set out to create. If you haven’t already, come join our Discord community to share your thoughts and hear what others uncovered in the streets of Retropolis.

reviews.review_description:
After having played Floor Plan, Floor Plan 2, The Secret of Retropolis and its sequel I’d rank  Peanut Button as one of the top VR developers.

Their games are consistently well crafted. The art direction, dialog, and puzzle design are all top tier stuff.

I called its prequel “short and sweet”. This sequel is even sweeter and definitely not short unless you binge on games like some people binge Netflix.  If you’re someone who burns through an entire season of a Netflix series in a couple of marathon sessions then this game may be “too short”.  If you’re the type who watches an episode a night you should be more than satisfied with the length.

Reply to the developers:

I thought your studio made the Floor Plan games. I got confused by the similarity between your studio’s name and the other “Button” studio, Turbo Button.

I realized my mistake when I visited your website to look for info on your upcoming game. I’m really happy it’s a horror themed game!

Another good comparison to Retropolis I could have made is Monkey Island. No chance of confusing the names Peanut Button and LucasArts. 😁

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, Jioxx—what an incredible compliment! Being mentioned alongside titles like Floor Plan and Floor Plan 2 truly means a lot to us, and we’re so glad Retropolis resonated with you.

Your take on pacing is spot on—we love the idea of savoring each chapter like a good show. We’re currently working on a brand-new game in a new franchise, and we’d love to have you along for the ride. Join our Discord or mailing list to catch early updates and see what’s coming next!

reviews.review_description: Loved it!  Great storyline, artwork, and puzzles !

developer_response.body: Thank you, FieryGoldfish5794! We’re thrilled you connected with the story, art, and puzzles—those are the pillars we built Never Say Goodbye on. We’re cooking up more narrative-driven adventures and would love to have you along for the ride. Join our Discord or mailing list to see what’s next in store!

reviews.review_title: Not as good as the first one
reviews.review_description: ￼ I guess I am in the minority here, but I did not enjoy this game as much as the first one. The puzzles are not easy, some of them were so difficult for me that I had to rely on online walk-through to be able to solve them. Also, some of them just did not make sense. The worst part is that there is no ending to this game. It is just a segway for the next one. The first one had a complete story from start to finish. I cannot recommend this game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts, Botafogo2022. We’re really sorry to hear that Never Say Goodbye didn’t land for you the way the first game did. We understand that puzzle difficulty can feel different for everyone, and we appreciate you sticking with it even when it got tough. Your feedback on the puzzles and the open-ended story is something we take seriously as we continue building the world of Retropolis.

If you ever feel like sharing more about which puzzles felt off or too tricky, we’d love to hear from you—whether through our support email or Discord community. Either way, we truly appreciate you giving both games a try and sharing your honest experience.

reviews.review_description:
This game just gets better and better..hoping for a next instalment 🤞 
Loved the first game, and was wow’d by this one! The story continues which is great, and nice to see a cross over of characters from the first game. The twists and turns, and superb interactive sections make it the ultimate game. Thank you again!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, flavourlabel.2024! It’s amazing to hear how much the journey through Retropolis has kept surprising you—that really means the world to us. We’re so glad the story, characters, and twists all hit the mark for you!

We’ve got more in the works, so fingers crossed 🤞 won’t be necessary! 😉 If you want to stay updated, feel free to join our Discord or mailing list—we’d love to have you along for what’s next! 🚀

reviews.review_description: You can tell the developers made this game with love. Love the references to the first game (although not necessary to play the first one, I am glad I did), the humour, and the improvement in graphics and details from the first game. The story and writing were hilarious and so well done. Please please keep making games. Can’t wait to see what else you guys come up with!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, SKNYVR! It means the world to us that you felt the love and care we put into Retropolis 2. We’re so glad you enjoyed the humor, story, and all the little nods to the first game—it’s always special when returning players catch those details!

And don’t worry, we *are* making more! If you’re curious about what’s next, join our Discord or mailing list—we’re already sharing the journey there. Thanks again for your amazing support! 😊🎭🚀

reviews.review_description: I’m waiting for the third game! Again a beautiful story and world building!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, vegan_big_daddy! It means a lot to know the story and world of Retropolis stayed with you. We absolutely plan to return and share the rest of the story—we’re not done with those robots yet!

Right now, we’re working on a game in a new franchise, and we’d love to have you along for the ride. Join our Discord or mailing list to stay connected and catch what’s coming next! 🚀

reviews.review_description: Wow, what a delightful game! It builds beautifully on the first installment, refining every aspect with great puzzles, captivating locations, and an engaging story. While the original was a bit short, this sequel is at least twice, if not three times, larger. If you enjoy a good adventure game, don’t miss out on this gem!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, banjoguy! We’re thrilled to hear how much you enjoyed the sequel and appreciated the expanded story and refined gameplay. Knowing it built on what you loved in the original means a lot to us. Thanks for the glowing recommendation, and we hope to see you in our community for more adventures! 😊

reviews.review_description:
Everything was great. Controls, music, dialogs, and story. I was very entertained for two days! What do I do now? Make another one!!!! 
Only complaint is the blue color filter. I have some macula thing with my retina. Got the neighbor to do the first code. Anyways the safe opened. 
Thank you for your beautiful creation! Seriously, make more!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much, Candicebarr! It’s amazing to hear that you had such a great time with the game, all the way through to that very intense finale! We’re especially touched that you went the extra mile to get past the blue filter challenge—teamwork makes the safe open! 😄

And hearing that you want more? That means the world to us. We’re working hard on another adventure, and we’d love to keep you in the loop—feel free to join our Discord or mailing list to stay updated on what’s next! 👻
